name: ICNA Council for Social Justice
mission: To systematically facilitate assertive Muslim involvement in the field of human struggle for the rights of the poor and oppressed.
scoreSummary: Icna Council for Social Justice shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.
zakatClaimEvidence: ICNA Council for Social Justice is listed as "Zakat eligible" on Feeling Blessed. Their website also features a "Zakat Calculator - ICNA CSJ" and mentions "Zakat Qualification". Furthermore, they explicitly discuss how zakat should be given in America to the poor. (Source: https://www.feelingblessed.org/icnacsj)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 20.6% of revenue ($157,116 on $763,582)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high donor fit for the Muslim community as a Zakat-eligible entity focused on religious and cultural advocacy [2]. Its mission to support the oppressed aligns with urgent social justice causes, though its track record is primarily viewed through its capacity-building efforts [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates a commitment to its mission with a total revenue of $763,582 and a program expense ratio of 82.6% [1][3]. While it maintains strong financial health and a Charity Navigator score of 86.5/100, there is a moderate level of data confidence regarding specific program effectiveness [1].

strengths: High program expense ratio of 82.6%, ensuring the majority of funds reach core initiatives
Strong fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.10 per $1 raised
Maintains a healthy 7.3 months of working capital for operational stability

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support Muslim-led social justice advocacy and human rights education through a highly efficient, zakat-eligible vehicle.

Value organizations with extremely low administrative overhead
Prioritize systemic change and capacity building over direct aid
giving_considerations: The organization is in a growth phase with a 22.7% CAGR
Focus is on domestic US advocacy and social justice education
Executive compensation is higher than average for this revenue tier
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-volume direct humanitarian aid (e.g., food parcels) or those requiring rigorous RCT-level impact data.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes in advocacy but a <cite id="1">lack of specific beneficiary counts</cite> to measure cost-effectiveness.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ICNA CSJ creates impact by facilitating <cite id="1">assertive Muslim involvement</cite> in human rights struggles through education and systematic advocacy.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts translate into tangible policy changes or community empowerment.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="3">22.7% revenue growth rate</cite> and expanding advocacy needs, the organization has significant capacity to absorb new funding.
strategic_priorities: Systematic Muslim involvement in human rights

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="1">exceptionally high 82.6% program expense ratio</cite> compared to broader advocacy peers.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="1">82.6% of its total expenses toward programs</cite>, which is significantly higher than the industry standard for advocacy groups.
ICNA CSJ demonstrates high fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="1">$0.09 to raise every $1 in contributions</cite>.
The charity maintains <cite id="1">7.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a strong buffer for long-term operational sustainability.

strengths_deep_dive: ICNA CSJ's financial health is anchored by its <cite id="1">overall score of 86.86.5/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, which highlights its accountability and leadership. The organization's leadership is particularly strong, earning a <cite id="1">perfect score of 100 in the Leadership & Adaptability beacon</cite>. This is supported by a <cite id="3">100% independent board of directors</cite>, ensuring that governance is free from internal conflicts of interest. Furthermore, the organization has shown consistent growth, with <cite id="3">net assets increasing to $465,487 in 2023</cite>, up from $306,271 in 2021, reflecting a stable upward trajectory in its capacity to fund social justice work.
rich_narrative.summary: The ICNA Council for Social Justice (ICNA CSJ) serves as a dedicated platform for <cite id="1">systematic Muslim involvement in human rights advocacy</cite> and social justice initiatives. Operating as a specialized branch of the Islamic Circle of North America, the organization focuses on empowering the poor and oppressed through <cite id="1">capacity building and social justice education</cite>. Financially, the organization demonstrated significant growth in its most recent filing, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $834,440</cite>, which represents a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 22.7%</cite>. A defining characteristic of ICNA CSJ is its exceptional commitment to direct program funding; the organization maintains a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 82.6%</cite>, ensuring that nearly every dollar donated is utilized for its core mission rather than administrative overhead. Furthermore, the organization is <cite id="2">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations through programs that serve the <cite id="2">fuqara, masakin, and fisabilillah</cite> categories. With <cite id="1">7.3 months of working capital</cite>, the organization maintains a resilient financial position to sustain its advocacy efforts.
